tldr: Move from OneSignal's developer-friendly push platform to Braze's enterprise customer engagement suite with Canvas journey orchestration.
why_switch: Need cross-channel journey orchestration (push, in-app, email, SMS, WhatsApp) in one place
Want richer segmentation, personalization, and lifecycle tooling
Require enterprise SLAs and account management
Outgrowing OneSignal's campaign UI for complex marketing flows
Consolidate engagement under a single CRM-style platform
what_to_export: User / device records with external IDs and tags
Push tokens and channel info (for reference; reregister via SDK)
Templates per channel (push, email, in-app, SMS)
Custom user properties and event taxonomy
Brand / styling assets used in templates
export_method: Use the OneSignal API to export users (CSV exports for users are typically available) and pull templates and segments via the API. Confirm exact endpoints, export formats, and rate limits in OneSignal's official documentation before scripting.
import_method: Integrate the Braze SDKs into iOS/Android/Web apps so users register through Braze and push tokens are captured fresh. Use Braze's REST API (User Track / Users endpoints) to upload historical attributes and events. Rebuild templates, segments, and Canvas journeys inside the Braze dashboard. Verify endpoint names, payload shape, and rate limits in Braze's official documentation before automating bulk imports.

concept_mapping.note: Map your stable user id 1:1 to keep identity consistent
Convert string/numeric tags to typed Braze attributes
Recreate per channel; consider Content Blocks for reuse
Multi-step journeys live in Canvas with branching and delays
Standardize event names and properties before importing

gotchas: Push tokens cannot be transferred — devices must reregister through the Braze SDK after release
Lock-in increases significantly — Braze accumulates rich profile data that is harder to extract later
Pricing jumps from freemium to enterprise contracts; negotiate quotas and overages up front
Identity unification rules differ; plan anonymous-to-known merge behavior carefully
Event taxonomy should be cleaned up before import — bad names will haunt segmentation
Run dual-send during cutover and validate deliverability across each channel before disabling OneSignal

email.subject: Migrating from OneSignal to Braze
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move our engagement stack from OneSignal to Braze so we can run cross-channel journeys (push, in-app, email, SMS, WhatsApp) through Canvas with stronger segmentation and enterprise support.

Plan:
- Export users, tags, segments, templates, and automations from OneSignal
- Integrate Braze SDKs in iOS/Android/Web; devices reregister organically
- Upload historical attributes and events via Braze's REST API
- Rebuild segments, templates, and Canvas journeys
- Dual-send during cutover, validate deliverability per channel, then turn OneSignal off

Estimated effort: 40-120 hours depending on journey complexity and event taxonomy cleanup. Detailed checklist to follow.

Thanks
